[Incredible Luck](/Feats.aspx?ID=6178) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=825" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 169

**Prerequisites**
[Charmed Life](/Feats.aspx?ID=6138)

</column>

---

You always seem to bounce back from the worst. When you use Charmed Life, roll the save twice (including the +2 circumstance bonus from Charmed Life) and use the better result.
name: Incredible Luck

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 169 **Prerequisites** precise strike 6d6 --- You stab your foe in a vital organ, possibly killing them outright. Make a Strike. On a success, you forego your precise strike damage from the finisher. Instead, your target takes additional precision damage based on a Fortitude save against your class DC. If your Strike was a critical hit, the target's saving throw outcome is one degree worse. **Critical Success** You deal 6 precision damage.
**Success** You deal 6d6 precision damage.
**Failure** You deal 12d6 precision damage.
**Critical Failure** You deal 18d6 precision damage.
name: Lethal Finisher

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Parry and Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=6180) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=825" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 169

**Prerequisites**
Opportune Riposte

</column>

---

Your parries and finishers allow you to riposte with the slightest provocation. You can use Opportune Riposte against an enemy who fails at a Strike against you (not just critically fails), provided you have both damaged that creature with a [finisher](/Traits.aspx?ID=802) on your last turn and currently have a circumstance bonus to AC from the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) weapon trait, or [Extravagant Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=6128).
name: Parry and Riposte

<title

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Illimitable Finisher](/Feats.aspx?ID=6181) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Finisher" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=802" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=825" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 169

</column>

---

Your strikes flow endlessly. Step and attempt a single one-action [finisher](/Traits.aspx?ID=802). This Strike gains the [bravado](/Traits.aspx?ID=801) trait, possibly replenishing your panache; if you regain panache, you can immediately attempt another finisher as part of this action, ignoring the restriction of using actions with the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) trait after a finisher.
name: Illimitable Finisher

    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6184) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=659" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 175

**Archetype** [Alchemist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=228)

**Prerequisites**
Intelligence +2

</column>

---

You put your alchemical interest into practice. You become trained in alchemical bombs, the alchemist class DC, and Crafting; if you were already trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

You gain the [Quick Alchemy](/Rules.aspx?ID=3282) benefits, creating up to 4 versatile vials during your daily preparations. Add the formulas for four additional common alchemical items to your formula book, in addition to those you gain from [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117).

<title level="2">Alchemist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Advanced Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=6185), [Alchemical Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=6189), [Basic Concoction](/Feats.aspx?ID=6186), [Voluminous Vials](/Feats.aspx?ID=6188)
name: Alchemist Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Champion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6196) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=659" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 177

**Archetype** [Champion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=230)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma +2; Strength +2

</column>

---

Choose a deity. You are bound by your deity’s anathema and gain the champion’s aura and sanctification as described in the [champion](/Classes.aspx?ID=58) class. Choose a cause as you would if you were a champion, with the same options a champion must abide by. You gain its edicts and anathema but don’t gain the other abilities.

You become trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) and your deity’s associated skill; for each of these skills in which you were already trained, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You become trained in champion class DC. If you later gain a devotion spell, you become trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC. 

You become trained in light armor and medium armor. If you already were trained in light armor and medium armor, you gain training in heavy armor as well. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in any type of armor (but not unarmored defense), you also gain that proficiency in the armor types granted to you by this feat. If you have a class feature that grants you expert proficiency in unarmored defense and you’re 13th level or higher, you also become an expert in the armor types granted to you by this feat.

Charisma +2

</column>

---

Choose a mystery. You become trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) and the mystery’s skill; if you were already trained, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You gain the curse associated with your mystery, which follows the normal rules for an oracular curse.

You cast spells like an oracle and gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity. You gain a spell repertoire with two cantrips, either common divine cantrips or other divine cantrips you learn or discover. You’re trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for oracle archetype spells is Charisma, and they are divine oracle spells.

<title level="2">Oracle Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Mysteries](/Feats.aspx?ID=6218), [Basic Oracle Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=6219), [First Revelation](/Feats.aspx?ID=6220)

Choose a bloodline. You become trained in the bloodline’s two skills; for each of these skills in which you were already trained, you become trained in a skill of your choice.

You gain a spell repertoire with two common cantrips from the spell list associated with your bloodline, from the spells granted by your bloodline, or any other cantrips of that tradition you learn or discover. You’re trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for sorcerer archetype spells is Charisma, and they are sorcerer spells of your bloodline’s tradition. You don’t gain any other abilities from your choice of bloodline.

<title level="2">Sorcerer Dedication leads to...</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 183 **Archetype** [Acrobat](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=236) **Prerequisites** [Acrobat Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6240) **Requirements** You are adjacent to an enemy. --- You deftly dive through a gap left by a foe to deliver an advantageous blow. Attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check against the Reflex DC of an enemy adjacent to you. **Critical Success** You move through the enemy’s space to an unoccupied space on the other side of the enemy from your starting position. This movement doesn’t trigger reactions. You can’t move farther than your Speed, and you must end your movement adjacent to the enemy whose space you moved through. After moving, you make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the enemy whose space you moved through, and the enemy is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against that Strike.
**Success** As critical success, but the enemy isn’t off-guard against the Strike.
**Failure** You remain in your original space but can still Strike.
**Critical Failure** You neither move nor Strike.
name: Tumbling Strike
requirement_markdown: You are adjacent to an enemy.

</column>

---

You've trained to assassinate your foes, and you do so with tenacity and precision. You gain the Mark for Death activity. 

 ** Mark for Death** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** The creature you intend to mark is [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) by you; **Effect** You designate a single creature as your mark. Using Mark for Death while hidden or undetected doesn't make you observed. Mark for Death lasts until the mark dies or you use Mark for Death again. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to Seek your mark, as well as to Deception checks to Feint against your mark. Your mark takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all Perception checks to Seek you.

 In addition, when attacking your mark, you have the [sneak attack](/Classes.aspx?ID=37) class feature, except it deals 1d4 precision damage and you don't increase the number of dice as you gain levels. At 6th level, the damage increases to 1d6. If you already have the sneak attack class feature, you instead deal an additional 1 precision damage with your sneak attacks made against your mark, increasing to 2 precision damage at 6th level.

<title level="2">Assassin Dedication leads to...</title>
[Angel of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=6260), [Assassinate](/Feats.aspx?ID=6261), [Expert Backstabber](/Feats.aspx?ID=6258), [Surprise Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=6259)
name: Assassin Dedication

[Beastmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6267) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 188

**Archetype** [Beastmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=241)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

You gain the service of a young animal companion that travels with you and obeys your commands. Contrary to the usual rules for animal companions, this feat can grant you a second animal companion. If you ever have more than one animal companion, you gain the Call Companion action.

Certain beastmaster feats give you focus spells. When you gain your first beastmaster focus spell, you become trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for beastmaster archetype spells is Charisma, and they are primal spells. You can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) by feeding, playing with, or otherwise tending to an animal companion.

<title level="2">Beastmaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Additional Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=6268), [Beastmaster Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=6273), [Beastmaster's Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=6274), [Beastmaster's Trance](/Feats.aspx?ID=6270), [Mature Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=6269), [Swift Guardian](/Feats.aspx?ID=6271)

<title level="3">Beastmaster Animal Companions</title> If you're playing a beastmaster, you determine the statistics and abilities of your [animal companions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2113). As a beastmaster, it's possible for you to have more than one animal companion at one time—up to four companions—but only one of those companions, your “active companion,” follows you during exploration and in encounters; the rest are nearby, usually foraging or hunting for food. As soon as you gain a second animal companion from the Beastmaster archetype, you also gain Call Companion to switch your active companion. These rules apply to all your companions, regardless of whether you got the animal companion from the beastmaster archetype or from another source.

<document level="2" id="action-2900" />
name: Beastmaster Dedication

</column>

---

All of your animal companions grow up, becoming [mature animal companions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2117) and gaining additional capabilities.

Your animal companion has greater independence. During an encounter, even if you don't use the [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) action, your active animal companion can still use 1 action that round on your turn to Strike or Stride (or [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) if it has that Speed).. It can do this at any point during your turn, as long as you aren't currently taking an action. If it does, that's all the actions it gets that round— you can't Command it later.

<title level="2">Mature Beastmaster Companion leads to...</title>
[Incredible Beastmaster Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=6272), [Lead the Pack](/Feats.aspx?ID=6276)

---

When focused on finding your quarry, you’re relentless. You gain the [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=2257) action. You can use Hunt Prey to designate a creature that you’ve observed, heard about, or learned about through some other means, such as a bounty board or wanted poster. In addition to the other benefits of Hunt Prey, you can designate a target as your prey while Gathering Information about them, in addition to designating them as your prey when Tracking them as normal. If you have already identified your target and selected them as your prey, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Gather Information about them.

If you already have Hunt Prey, you gain the [Monster Hunter](/Feats.aspx?ID=4863) feat in addition to the other benefits of this feat.

<title level="2">Bounty Hunter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Keep Pace](/Feats.aspx?ID=6284), [Opportunistic Grapple](/Feats.aspx?ID=6285), [Posse](/Feats.aspx?ID=6282), [Tools of the Trade](/Feats.aspx?ID=6283)

<title

>
[Cavalier Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6286) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 192

**Archetype** [Cavalier](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=244)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)

</column>

---

You gain a young animal companion that serves as your mount. You can choose from animal companions with the mount special ability, as well as any additional options from your pledge (see Cavalier Pledges sidebar), as determined by your GM. You must choose an animal companion that’s at least one size larger than you, but if the animal usually starts as Small, you can begin with a Medium version of that animal (changing no statistics other than its size).

**Special** If you have pledged yourself to a cause, you can take a second dedication feat closely tied to that cause even if you haven’t taken two additional cavalier feats. For instance, if you pledged yourself to fight crime with a group of vigilantes, you could take the vigilante dedication without first completing the cavalier archetype. The GM determines what archetypes, if any, are valid choices.

<title level="2">Cavalier Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cavalier's Banner](/Feats.aspx?ID=6287), [Cavalier's Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=6288), [Cushion Landing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7986), [Dashing Pickup](/Feats.aspx?ID=7988), [Defend Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=6291), [Impressive Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=6289), [Legendary Rider](/Feats.aspx?ID=6297), [Mounted Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=6292), [Mounting Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=7987), [Quick Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=6290), [Rearing Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=7990), [Trampling Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=6294), [Unseat](/Feats.aspx?ID=6295), [Vaulting Gallop](/Feats.aspx?ID=7989)

<title level="3">Cavalier Pledges</title> Many cavaliers pledge themselves in service to a specific organization or individual, such as a noble family or sovereign ruler, a knightly order or another organization, or particular ideal. A cavalier pledged to an order or ideal must uphold its edicts and is beholden to any anathema it has; for example, a cavalier pledged to the druid's animal order would be tied to that anathema, while a cavalier pledged to the church of Abadar would be held to the same standards as a cleric of that god. If you pledge yourself to an entity or cause without a stated anathema, you should work with your GM to determine the edicts and anathema associated with your pledge. Cavaliers who have pledged themselves can gain access to certain abilities related to that pledge, such as Cavalier's Banner. If you violate the anathema of your pledge, you lose access to those feats until you benefit from an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual. Changing the subject of your pledge is equivalent to retraining a class feature.
name: Cavalier Dedication

You command your mount to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to double its Speed (or to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), if it has the corresponding movement type), moving through the spaces of any foes in your path up to one size smaller than your mount. Your mount deals the damage of one of its melee [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895) to each creature whose space you move through, with a basic Reflex save against your mount’s [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) DC. On a critical failure, the creature also becomes off-guard until the end of your next turn. Roll the damage only once. You can damage a given creature only once during this movement.

You attend all the fashionable society parties, no matter how exclusive they might be. Any time you encounter a social event you would ordinarily be denied access to, such as a coronation, royal gala, or other society function, you can spend 1d4 hours to secure entry without the need for a skill check. You find invitations, invitees looking for fashionable dates, temporary jobs with the caterers, event staff willing to look the other way, or some other mode of access for yourself and your allies. This ability doesn’t apply to secret events or other small private gatherings with no staff, dates, or outsiders involved.

<traits>

<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />

**Prerequisites**
expert in at least one weapon from the or [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) or [crossbow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17) weapon group

</column>

---

You channel powerful magic with your archery, allowing you to deliver potent spells through the tip of an arrow rather than the more mundane flick of the wrist or whatever other gestures are usually used. If you don't already cast spells from spell slots, you learn to cast spontaneous spells and gain the Cast a Spell activity. You gain a spell repertoire with one cantrip of your choice, from a tradition of your choice. You choose a common cantrip or other cantrip to which you have access. This cantrip must require a spell attack roll and come from your chosen tradition. You're trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC. Your key spellcasting attribute for these spells is Charisma.

 Regardless of whether you can already cast spells or gained the ability from this dedication, you gain the Eldritch Shot activity.

 **Eldritch Shot** <actions string="Three Actions" /> **Requirements** You are wielding a bow or a loaded crossbow; **Effect** You Cast a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack roll. The effects of the spell do not occur immediately but are imbued into the bow you're wielding. Make a Strike with that bow. Your spell flies with the ammunition, using your attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for your multiple attack penalty, but you don't apply the penalty until after you've completed the Eldritch Shot. Combine the Strike and spell's damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

<title level="2">Eldritch Archer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Eldritch Archer Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=6318), [Eldritch Reload](/Feats.aspx?ID=6322), [Enchanting Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6319), [Fatal Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6326), [Homing Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6324), [Incorporeal Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6325), [Magic Ammunition](/Feats.aspx?ID=6320), [Precious Ammunition](/Feats.aspx?ID=6321)
name: Eldritch Archer Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: expert in at least one weapon from the or [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) or [crossbow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17) weapon group

[Magic Ammunition](/Feats.aspx?ID=6320) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 199

**Prerequisites**
[Eldritch Archer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6317)

</column>

---

You imbue your ammunition with raw and potent magic, transforming it to suit your needs in any situation. When you select this feat, choose three types of [common magical ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=16) of 4th level or lower from _GM Core_ or this book. Your GM might allow you to choose from other types of magical ammunition of an appropriate level, such as [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721) ammunition, or ammunition from other books. You gain the Transform Ammunition action.

 **Transform Ammunition** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** You transform a non-magical arrow or bolt on your person into a piece of magical ammunition of one type you chose for the Magic Ammunition feat. You must shoot the ammunition before the end of your turn or the magic dissipates. If the ammunition has an Activate entry, you still need to spend the required actions to activate the ammunition before shooting it. You can choose a type of magical ammunition that is typically not available to the type of ammunition you're using—for example, you can use climbing bolt on an arrow, even though that magical ammunition is normally only found on bolts.

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times. Each time you do, select three additional types of magical ammunition as described above.
name: Magic Ammunition

You become an expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) and can use Nature instead of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) alchemical consumables with the healing trait. **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 202 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For Pathfinder Society play, Endemic Herbs does not have the additive trait._ **Archetype** [Herbalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=252) **Prerequisites** [Herbalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6339); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) --- The herbal items you create use medicinal plants endemic to your location. When a creature consumes one of your herbal items, that creature gains the benefit matching the location where you created that item. If the benefit is not immediate, it lasts for 1 minute unless otherwise stated.
  • **Aquatic** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves.
  • **Arctic** For 1 hour, treat [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2769) as if they were one step less severe.
  • **Desert** For 1 hour, treat environmental heat effects as if they were one step less severe.
  • **Forest** Gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) effects.
  • **Mountain** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves.
  • **Plains** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves.
  • **Swamp** Remove one source of [persistent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) bleed damage.
  • **Underground** Gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298).
name: Endemic Herbs

    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Read Shibboleths](/Feats.aspx?ID=6349) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 203

**Archetypes** [Linguist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=253), [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) (Level 7)

**Prerequisites**
[Linguist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6344); Master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)

</column>

---

All communication is filled with slight tells and signals. If you interact with someone for at least 10 minutes, their regional words, pronunciation changes, and nonverbal cues provide you with a minor fact about their social environment, such as their hometown or certain groups they might belong to. If a target of this ability is being deceptive about their social environment, such as a commoner pretending to be a noble, they secretly attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47), or appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check against your Society DC, providing you a false minor fact consistent with their assumed identity on a success.

Once you’ve gleaned one or more true minor facts about a person, you can then incorporate these mannerisms into your speech to present yourself in a way that they find familiar. This grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to your [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and Deception checks with them.
name: Read Shibboleths

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crude Communication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6350) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 203

**Archetypes** [Linguist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=253), [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) (Level 8)

**Prerequisites**
[Linguist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6344)

</column>

---

Even if you don’t speak a creature’s language, you can rely on inflection, root words, and body language to infer rudimentary meaning. If you interact with a creature for at least 10 minutes and that creature can speak a language, the GM rolls a secret [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) check for you, with a DC appropriate for the language’s rarity. On a success, you understand the gist of the meaning and can communicate basic concepts back to the creature; on a failure, you are mistaken or communicate incorrect concepts. If you’re legendary in Society, you can communicate instantly without needing to attempt a Society check; even if you didn’t know the medium of communication the creature uses (speech, sign language, and so on), you intuit this information as soon as they try to communicate.
name: Crude Communication

Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); trained in martial weapons

</column>

---

You’ve combined your social skills and combat training to become a talented combat leader. Choose [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40). You become trained in that skill or become an expert if you were already trained in it.

In addition, while you’re conscious, you’re surrounded by a marshal’s aura in a 15-foot emanation. Your aura has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) traits and grants you and allies in the aura a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598).

<title level="2">Marshal Dedication leads to...</title>
[Back to Back](/Feats.aspx?ID=6358), [Cadence Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=6356), [Coordinated Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=6361), [Dread Marshal Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=6352), [Form Up!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7018), [General's Gambit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7019), [Inspiring Marshal Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=6353), [Know Your Enemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=7017), [Rallying Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=6357), [Snap Out of It!](/Feats.aspx?ID=6354), [Steel Yourself!](/Feats.aspx?ID=6355), [Strategist Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7016), [Tactical Cadence](/Feats.aspx?ID=6362), [Target of Opportunity](/Feats.aspx?ID=6363), [To Battle!](/Feats.aspx?ID=6359), [Topple Foe](/Feats.aspx?ID=6360)

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 205

**Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=254)

**Prerequisites**
[Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6351)

**Trigger**
An ally succeeds at a ranged  [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against an opponent in your weapon’s first range increment.

**Requirements**
You have a ranged or thrown weapon in hand.

</column>

---

You capitalize on your ally’s attack and use the opportunity to secure a blow of your own, adding to the oncoming barrage. Make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with a –2 penalty against the opponent targeted by the triggering attack. This Strike doesn’t count toward your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289), and your multiple attack penalty doesn’t apply to this Strike. If this Strike is successful, combine the damage from the attack with the damage from your ally’s attack for the purpose of [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) and [weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317).

Treating a Condition that is continually applied under certain circumstances (for instance, the enfeebled condition a [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) character gains from carrying a weapon with the [_unholy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851) rune) has no effect as long as the circumstances continue. **Critical Success** Reduce the condition value by 2.
**Success** Reduce the condition value by 1.
**Critical Failure** Increase the condition value by 1. Treat Condition leads to... [Holistic Care](/Feats.aspx?ID=6376)

You resuscitate the dead. Attempt a DC 40 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check to revive a dead creature who has been dead for no more than 3 rounds and whose body is mostly intact. If you succeed, the target returns to life with the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), except it still has the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition it had before dying, increased by 1 (or wounded 1 if it wasn’t wounded before dying). Whether you succeed or fail, the target is temporarily immune to Resuscitate for 1 day.
